title: Show HN: Semantic search across 40k+ AI agents (vs keyword matching)
content:
We built semantic search for AI agent discovery because keyword search fails when you search for "customer support" but agents are tagged as "helpdesk" or "user assistance."

The system uses embeddings to understand that "financial analysis" matches agents tagged as "trading algorithms" or "risk assessment." We've indexed 40k+ agents from GitHub, npm, PyPI, HuggingFace.

Try it: https://agentcrawl.dev
API: https://api.agentcrawl.dev/docs

Technical details: We process agent descriptions, READMEs, and usage examples through sentence transformers, then use FAISS for sub-100ms semantic similarity search. Trust scoring combines maintenance activity, community adoption, and stability metrics.

What's your experience with agent discovery? Any interesting matching problems you've solved?

strategy_notes.engagement_approach: Focus on technical problem, ask engaging question
strategy_notes.expected_impact: +400 weekly visitors if front page success
strategy_notes.follow_up_plan: Monitor first 2 hours, respond to technical questions
strategy_notes.success_metrics: Stay in top 30 for 4+ hours, 20+ comments, 1000+ visitors
